metadata.name: Cloak: Cryptic Coat ETB cloaks top library card, then turn face up
metadata.description: p1 casts Cryptic Coat ({2}{U}), an Artifact Equipment whose ETB trigger cloaks the top card of p1's library (Llanowar Elves). The cloaked permanent enters the battlefield as a face-down 2/2 with ward {2} (CR 701.58 — Cloak differs from Manifest in that it grants ward {2} while face-down). p1 then pays Llanowar Elves' mana cost {G} to turn it face up, revealing it as a 1/1 creature. Tests CR 701.58 (Cloak action), CR 702.37 (face-down permanent rules), and CR 702.17 (ward on face-down Cloaked permanent).
metadata.generation_notes: Cryptic Coat: {2}{U}, Artifact Equipment, card_id 'cryptic-coat'. ETB trigger: 'When this Equipment enters, cloak the top card of your library.' Cloak (CR 701.58) puts the top card face-down as a 2/2 creature with ward {2} — this is the key difference from Manifest (CR 701.40), which does not grant ward. Llanowar Elves ({G}, 1/1 creature) is pre-placed in p1's library. Mana pool setup: {colorless: 2, blue: 1, green: 1} to cover Cryptic Coat cast + Llanowar Elves turn-face-up cost. After Cryptic Coat resolves (enters battlefield), the ETB trigger queues on the stack (CR 603.3). After priority round, the trigger resolves and Cloak action puts Llanowar Elves face-down on battlefield as a 2/2. Face-down permanents have no name (CR 708.2a) so cannot be asserted by card name — confirmed indirectly by library becoming empty and battlefield creature count increasing. Ward {2} on the face-down permanent is a Cloak-specific characteristic per CR 701.58b. After turn_face_up with method 'mana_cost' paying {G}, Llanowar Elves is revealed as a 1/1 and becomes visible by name on the battlefield. The Cryptic Coat Equipment itself remains on the battlefield (it has its own ETB, does not sacrifice itself).

script.step_note: p1 casts Cryptic Coat; ETB trigger cloaks top library card (Llanowar Elves)

actions.description: Initial: Cryptic Coat in p1 hand, Llanowar Elves on top of p1 library, battlefield empty

actions.note: CR 601.2: p1 casts Cryptic Coat paying {2}{U}. No targets required. Card moves from hand to stack.

actions.description: After casting: Cryptic Coat on stack, hand empty
actions.note: Cryptic Coat spell is on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Cryptic Coat spell resolves.
CR 608.2: Cryptic Coat resolves and enters the battlefield as an artifact. ETB trigger queues on the stack per CR 603.3.

actions.description: After Cryptic Coat resolves: it is on the battlefield, ETB trigger on stack
actions.note: Cryptic Coat entered the battlefield. Its ETB trigger ('cloak the top card of your library') is now on the stack (CR 603.3).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — ETB trigger resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.58: Cloak puts the top card of p1's library face-down on the battlefield as a 2/2 creature with ward {2}. The cloaked card is Llanowar Elves.

actions.note: CR 608.2: ETB trigger resolves. Cloak action executed per CR 701.58 — Llanowar Elves is cloaked onto battlefield as a face-down 2/2 with ward {2}.

actions.description: After Cloak resolves: library empty, face-down 2/2 with ward {2} on p1 battlefield, stack empty
actions.note: Llanowar Elves is now face-down on the battlefield as a 2/2 with ward {2} (CR 701.58). It cannot be asserted by card name while face-down (CR 708.2a — face-down permanents have no name). Library count drops to 0 confirming the card was moved.

script.step_note: p1 turns the cloaked creature face up by paying Llanowar Elves' mana cost {G}

actions.note: CR 701.58: A cloaked permanent may be turned face up any time its controller has priority by paying its mana cost. p1 pays {G} (Llanowar Elves' mana cost) to turn it face up. This is a special action per CR 116.2b and does not use the stack.

actions.description: After turn face up: Llanowar Elves visible on p1 battlefield as a 1/1, Cryptic Coat also present
actions.note: CR 708.8: Face-up permanent now has its true characteristics. Llanowar Elves is a 1/1 green creature. Cryptic Coat Equipment remains on the battlefield. Ward {2} from Cloak no longer applies once the card is face-up (CR 701.58 — ward is a face-down characteristic).
